# servicenow_user (Resource)

`servicenow_user` manages a user record within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a user record within ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_user" "example" {
  user_name  = "jane.doe"
  first_name = "Jane"
  last_name  = "Doe"
  email      = "jane.doe@example.com"
  title      = "Software Engineer"
  active     = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `user_name` (String) The login user name for the user.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this user is active and able to log in.
- `company` (String) Sys ID of the company record the user belongs to.
- `department` (String) Sys ID of the department record the user belongs to.
- `email` (String) Email address of the user.
- `first_name` (String) First name of the user.
- `last_name` (String) Last name of the user.
- `location` (String) Sys ID of the location record the user is associated with.
- `locked_out` (Boolean) Whether or not the user account is locked out.
- `manager` (String) Sys ID of the user record that is this user's manager.
- `mobile_phone` (String) Mobile phone number of the user.
- `password_needs_reset` (Boolean) Whether the user must reset their password at next login.
- `phone` (String) Business phone number of the user.
- `time_zone` (String) Time zone preference for the user (for example 'US/Eastern').
- `title` (String) Business title of the user.
- `user_password` (String, Sensitive) Plain text password for the user. ServiceNow stores this value hashed and may not return the original; this field is write-only and not refreshed from state.
- `vip` (Boolean) Whether the user is flagged as a VIP.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_user.example <sys_id>
```
